'''Esan Marsters''' (born 17 August 1996) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]], [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Marsters was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of [[Cook Islands|Cook Island]] and [[Māori people|Māori]] descent.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2022/02/08/maori-experience-helping-marsters-grow-on-and-off-the-field/ |title=Maori experience helping Marsters grow on and off the field |publisher=Gold Coast Titans |date=8 February 2022 |access-date=13 March 2022}}</ref>

He played for [[Mount Albert Lions]] in New Zealand, later saying, "I played in the halves growing up. I was a five-eighth, like [[Benji Marshall|Benji]]. That’s where I get my ball skills from. Then I moved over to Sydney from Auckland when I was 17 and got switched to the centres. I was bigger than a lot of the other kids and they wanted to use me out wide."<ref>{{cite web| work= PlayersVoice | title=Benji, Ivan and Me| archive-date=14 March 2018|author=Esan Marsters | date=14 March 2018| url=https://www.playersvoice.com.au/esan-marsters-benji-ivan-me/#4hKCcfrPHv601KfW.97|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180314052432/https://www.playersvoice.com.au/esan-marsters-benji-ivan-me/}}</ref> Marsters picked up a contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]] to train with their [[SG Ball]] team. After spending a season with Roosters SG Ball team he was cut and signed a contract with the [[Wests Tigers]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-discard-earns-marsters-degree-in-perseverance-20180406-p4z85r.html |title=Roosters discard earns Marsters degree in perseverance |publisher=Sydney Morning Herald |date=6 April 2018 |access-date=13 March 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2015 and 2016, Marsters played for the Wests Tigers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.
[[File:Esan Marsters.jpg|right|thumb|Marsters playing for the Wests Tigers in 2015]]
Marsters played for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|2015 match against Tonga]]. In 2016 he represented the [[Junior Kiwis]] in a match against the Junior Kangaroos.<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2016/05/02/marsters_named_in_ju.html| title=Marsters named in Junior Kiwis squad| date=May 2016| access-date=17 August 2016}}</ref>

He graduated to the Wests Tigers' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] team in 2017.

===2017===
[[File:ESAN MARSTERS.jpg|right|thumb|Marsters playing for the Wests Tigers in 2017]]
On 6 May, Marsters represented the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in their [[2017 Pacific Rugby League Tests]] match.

In Round 13, Marsters made his [[NRL]] debut for the Wests Tigers against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], playing off the interchange bench in the 16–12 loss at [[ANZ Stadium]]. After playing on the bench for his first 2 games, he was in the starting team for the rest of the season, where he was described as, "a revelation at right centre".<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com | title=Why Marsters was born to win| author=Martin Gabor | date=8 August 2017| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/08/09/why-marsters-was-born-to-win/}}</ref> He scored 5 tries from 13 games in his rookie year.

===2018===
Marsters was said to be one of the most improved players in the game in 2018, and, "has been stellar in 2018, to the point he made his New Zealand Test debut and has claims on being one of the form centres right across the NRL."<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Stat Attack: The 17 most-improved players of 2018|author=Chris Kennedy | date=17 August 2018| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/17/stat-attack-the-17-most-improved-nrl-players-of-2018/}}</ref> He was one of two players to appear in every game for the Tigers and was their top point-scorer with 124.<ref>{{cite web| work= Rugby League Project |title=Wests Tigers 2018 | url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2018/wests-tigers/summary.html}}</ref> [[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]] named in their team of the year, saying, "he was the only centre to crack 3000 metres gained and his 126 metres per match was the biggest average of any centre who played at least 15 games. He also lead all centres in offloads with 49 and was fifth in tackle busts with 65."<ref>{{cite news| work= The Daily Telegraph| title=Team of the Year|page=94}}</ref>

===2019===
In round 4, Marsters missed all three attempts at goal in a 9–8 loss to the [[Penrith Panthers]], despite the [[Wests Tigers]] scoring two tries to Penrith's one. In round 9, Marsters scored two tries and kicked 3 goals in a 30–4 win over Penrith.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/wests-tigers-penrith-panthers-nrl-esan-marsters-nathan-cleary/v7wmnjdfwbrw1xgulgrefab9w/|title=Wests Tigers rue missed chances as Esan Marsters' goal-kicks go begging|website=Sporting News|date=5 April 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/panthers-hit-crisis-after-tigers-flogging-c-105585/|title=Panthers hit crisis after Tigers flogging|website=7 News|date=10 May 2019}}</ref> In Round 20, he scored two tries in a 28–4 win over [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] at [[Leichhardt Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-08-01/nrl-wests-tigers-beat-north-queensland-cowboys/11373050/|title=Wests Tigers beat North Queensland Cowboys 28-4 in NRL round 20|website=ABC|date=August 2019}}</ref>

On 10 October, the [[Wests Tigers]] released Marsters from the final year of his contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2019/10/10/esan-marsters-to-depart-wests-tigers/|title=Esan Marsters to depart Wests Tigers|date=10 October 2019}}</ref> On 14 October, he signed a three-year deal with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/10/14/cowboys-sign-kiwi-international-marsters/|title=Cowboys sign Kiwi international Marsters|date=14 October 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Marsters was a member of the North Queensland [[2020 NRL Nines]] winning squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/02/15/nines-final-team-list-final-v-dragons/|title=Nines final team list: Final v Dragons|date=15 February 2020}}</ref>

In round 1, he made his debut for North Queensland in their 21–28 loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/03/13/fifita-steals-the-show-as-broncos-spoil-cowboys-party/|title=Fifita's solo special helps Broncos spoil Cowboys party|date=13 March 2020}}</ref> In Round 6, he scored his first try for North Queensland in a 20–36 loss to the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2020/06/20/wests-tigers-start-fast-to-blow-away-cowboys/|title=Wests Tigers start fast to blow away Cowboys|date=20 June 2020|website=Wests Tigers}}</ref> Following their round 10 loss to the [[Penrith Panthers]], Marsters was dropped by interim head coach [[Josh Hannay]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/07/24/hannay-hopeful-cowboys-have-overcome-soft-underbelly/|title=Hannay hopeful Cowboys have overcome 'soft underbelly'|date=24 July 2020|website=NRL}}</ref> It was said, "Brought to Townsville to sharpen the Cowboys lacking backline and provide more attacking threats on the edges, Marsters struggled to live up to the hype. Defensive lapses plagued his start at the club, with his combination on the right side with Kyle Feldt the worst in the competition for conceding tries."<ref name=town>{{cite news| work= Townsville Bulletin | title=Cowboys coach Josh Hannay recalls Esan Marsters after honest conversations| author=Matthew Elkerton| url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/cowboys-coach-josh-hannay-recalls-esan-marsters-after-honest-conversations/news-story/3af0a2d80272741d45c52a964ff1b385}}</ref> He made just two more appearances for the North Queensland in 2020, scoring a try in their round 18 loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/news/best-and-worst-of-a-cowboys-recruit-seen-in-melbourne-storm-loss/news-story/ee73f0ff15371c8df75b9d5e95d05efd|title='Best and worst' of a Cowboys recruit seen in Melbourne Storm loss|date=13 September 2020|website=The Daily Telegraph}}</ref> Interim coach [[Josh Hannay]] explaining his absence by saying, "He is an extremely gifted player, Esan, but he can clock off at times out on the field. We have had some honest discussions around his work rate and consistency in that area."<ref name=town />

===2021===
On 4 May, after just three games for the North Queensland club in the [[2021 NRL season]], Marsters was released from his contract and signed with the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] until the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2021/05/04/marsters-becomes-a-titan/|title=Marsters becomes a Titan|date=4 May 2021|website=Gold Coast Titans}}</ref>

===2022===
On 17 September, Marsters signed a contract to join English side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-confirm-capture-nrl-25043961/|title=Huddersfield Giants confirm capture of NRL centre Esan Marsters as Ricky Leutele replacement|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
Marsters played 20 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs, Marsters scored his first Huddersfield try in a round 10 defeat to Hull FC.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
On 25 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]] on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=25 May 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Salford sign Huddersfield centre Marsters|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cyxxklp49kzo}}</ref>
Marsters played 22 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 9th on the table, despite this Marsters scored 8 tries and was impressive enough to earn a call up for the [[Cook Islands]] in the 2024 [[Rugby League Pacific championships]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
Marsters played 23 matches for Salford in the 2025 Super League season scoring five tries. Salford endured a horror year on and off the field as the club finished bottom of the Super League due to severe financial issues and limited player numbers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.prolificnorth.co.uk/news/struggling-salford-red-devils-hit-with-two-point-deduction-while-club-addresses-weekend-fan-protests/|title=Salford Red Devils dealt double blow as nightmare 2025 takes another turn|website=www.prolificnorth.co.uk}}</ref>

Marsters ran riot against [[South Africa Rugby League]] in the last round of the 2026 [[Rugby League World Cup]] qualifying match - captaining the side to a 58–6 win, coming up with 5 try assists and a try.

On 12 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Nov 2025|accessdate=12 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Salford hit with fresh transfer blow as fresh star departs for Championship rivals|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-hit-with-fresh-transfer-blow-as-fresh-star-departs-for-championship-rivals}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Marsters' cousin, [[Steven Marsters|Steven]], is a professional rugby league player for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/08/09/why-marsters-was-born-to-win/|title=Why Marsters was born to win|date=9 August 2017|website=NRL}}</ref>
